theme: designing a museum floorplan where visitors wander forever
title: The Endless Atrium
strategies: trivial: reproduce the checker's own compact near-square blob (baseline)
greedy: locally maximize graph diameter cell-by-cell up to the cap, then pad remaining cells wherever that keeps the diameter feasible (a distance-maximizing, conductance-blind recipe)

strong: exploit conductance-volume-balance -- when the diameter budget can hold all n cells as a straight corridor, use it (the extremal slow-mixing shape for a free diameter budget); otherwise decouple distance from volume via a hub-and-chamber (multi-throat 'dumbbell') layout: balanced compact 2D chambers, each attached through a single narrow throat to a short central hub, so the graph diameter only pays for chamber RADIUS (~sqrt(volume)) while conductance stays ~1/chamber-volume -- search over throat length and chamber count to find the best feasible layout under the cap
